metadata.desc: An intelligent long-term memory plugin with hybrid retrieval (BM25 + vector + graph), WebUI management, and dynamic lifecycle. Supports session/persona isolation, importance decay, and auto-cleanup.

bot_language.hint: Set the language for plugin command and status replies. zh=Chinese (default), en=English, ru=Russian.

provider_settings.hint: Configure the AI models used by the memory system

embedding_provider_id.hint: The Embedding model ID used to generate memory vectors. Leave blank to use AstrBot default. You can find existing Embedding Provider IDs in the AstrBot admin panel under 'LLM Configuration' (e.g., openai_embedding).

llm_provider_id.hint: The large language model ID used for summarizing conversations and evaluating memory importance. Leave blank to use AstrBot default. A model with strong reasoning capabilities is recommended.

session_manager.hint: Manage user session states and conversation history caching behavior

max_sessions.hint: Maximum number of sessions kept in memory (LRU eviction). Once exceeded, the least recently used session is removed from cache, but data remains in the database.

session_ttl.hint: Sessions inactive for longer than this duration are removed from cache. Default is 3600 (1 hour).

context_window_size.hint: Maximum number of historical messages passed to the LLM. Larger values provide richer context but consume more tokens. Recommended: 20-100.

max_messages_per_session.hint: Maximum number of historical messages retained per session in the database. When exceeded, only the oldest summarized messages are deleted to avoid losing unsummarized context.

cleanup_batch_size.hint: Minimum number of old summarized messages to delete per batch when the session history exceeds the limit. A value too small will cause only a few messages to be deleted each round after overflow.

recall_engine.hint: Control memory retrieval and injection into conversations

top_k.hint: Number of most relevant memories returned per retrieval. Set to 0 to skip automatic recall/injection while keeping historical injection cleanup. Recommended: 3-10.

max_k.hint: Maximum memories returned when the Agent actively calls the long-term memory retrieval tool. Used to limit single-tool recall scale. Recommended: 5-10.

importance_weight.hint: Weight coefficient for memory importance in the hybrid scoring. Higher values prioritize high-importance memories.

fallback_to_vector.hint: Automatically fall back to pure vector search when hybrid retrieval fails or returns empty results. Recommended to keep enabled.

injection_method.hint: Where to inject memories into LLM requests. user_message_before: as a user message prefix; system_prompt: append to system prompt; user_message_after: as a user message suffix; fake_tool_call: inject as a fake tool call message pair into conversation history, letting the LLM naturally understand this as retrieval results.

auto_remove_injected.hint: Automatically remove previously injected memory fragments from history before injecting new ones, preventing duplicate accumulation and token waste. Recommended to keep enabled.

importance_decay.hint: Gradually reduce the importance of old memories over time to prevent outdated information from retaining high weight indefinitely

decay_rate.hint: The proportion of memory importance decayed per day. 0.01 means a 1% decrease daily. Set to 0 to disable decay.

fusion_strategy.hint: Fusion parameters for BM25 and vector search results (internally uses the RRF algorithm)

rrf_k.hint: Lower values emphasize top-ranked results; higher values produce smoother fusion. Recommended: 30-120. Default is 60.

use_persona_filtering.hint: When enabled, only memories related to the current persona are recalled. Memories of different personas do not interfere with each other.

use_session_filtering.hint: When enabled, each session's memories are stored independently and not shared across sessions.

reflection_engine.hint: Control when conversation summarization is triggered and memories are generated

summary_trigger_rounds.hint: Trigger a summary and write it to memory after this many rounds of dialogue (one question and one answer counts as one round).

graph_memory.hint: When enabled, both document-based and graph-based paths are maintained simultaneously, each supporting keyword and vector retrieval, with results unified into a single fused recall set.

enabled.hint: When enabled, graph nodes, edges, and graph vector indexes are automatically built. Recommended to keep enabled.

document_route_weight.hint: The proportion of the document path fusion score in the final dual-path ranking.

graph_route_weight.hint: The proportion of the graph path fusion score in the final dual-path ranking.

cross_route_bonus.hint: Extra score added when the same memory is hit by both the document path and the graph path.

expansion_limit.hint: Maximum number of candidate entries expanded from hit nodes to their neighbors during graph keyword retrieval.

max_topics_per_memory.hint: Maximum number of topic nodes extracted from a single memory.

max_participants_per_memory.hint: Maximum number of participant nodes extracted from a single memory.

max_facts_per_memory.hint: Maximum number of key facts extracted from a single memory to enter the graph index.

migration_settings.hint: Control database version upgrade behavior on plugin startup

auto_migrate.hint: Automatically detect and upgrade outdated database versions on plugin startup. Recommended to keep enabled.

create_backup.hint: Automatically create a backup file before performing database migration to prevent data loss in case of migration failure. Recommended to keep enabled.

index_rebuild_settings.hint: Control batch size, Embedding request rate, and failure tolerance for the /lmem rebuild-index command. Low-spec cloud servers should keep concurrency small.

batch_size.hint: Number of memories read from the documents table per batch. Larger values increase memory peak and affect more entries on failure. Recommended: 25-100.

embedding_batch_size.hint: Number of texts included in a single Embedding API request. Reduce further if the provider has strict TPM rate limits.

tasks_limit.hint: Internal concurrency limit for batch Embedding. To avoid triggering API rate limits, the default is 1.

max_retries.hint: Maximum retry attempts for a failed Embedding batch.

retry_base_delay.hint: Base wait time for exponential backoff after a batch failure. At least 30 seconds will be waited when encountering 429/TPM rate limits.

batch_delay.hint: Additional wait time between each documents read batch, used to proactively reduce overall rebuild rate.

request_delay.hint: Wait time between each Embedding API sub-request. Increase this first if 429/TPM is triggered frequently.

max_failure_ratio.hint: During full vector rebuild, the new index will only be switched if the failure ratio does not exceed this value. 0.02 means up to 2% failure is allowed.

backup_settings.hint: Automatically back up the memory database daily to prevent accidental data loss

enabled.hint: Automatically back up the database after daily decay tasks. Recommended to keep enabled.

keep_days.hint: Backup files older than this number of days will be automatically deleted. Default is 7 days.

forgetting_agent.hint: Periodically clean up old memories that are both outdated and low importance to control memory store size

auto_cleanup_enabled.hint: Automatically clean up eligible old memories after daily importance decay.

cleanup_days_threshold.hint: Memories older than this many days become cleanup candidates. They will only be deleted if their importance is also below the threshold.

cleanup_importance_threshold.hint: Only memories with importance below this value will be cleaned up. 0.3 means memories below 30% importance.
